doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_add_d (mpfr/src/add_d.c L25-L50). Add an MPFR `b` to a machine double `c` and round to `prec` bits under `rnd`. The C reference is a trivial 25-line wrapper: it stack-allocates an mpfr_t with prec = IEEE_DBL_MANT_DIG (= 53), calls mpfr_set_d to fold the double into it (which is exact because 53 bits suffice for any binary64), then delegates to mpfr_add. The TS port mirrors this exactly: convert c to an MPFR via mpfr_set_d at prec=53 (exact for all finite, NaN, +/-Inf doubles), then delegate to mpfr_add(b, dMpfr, prec, rnd). The output ternary and rounded value come from mpfr_add; no further rounding. Class is 'misc' for the 1000ms budget; the implementation is O(1) modulo the inner mpfr_add cost.
c_signature: int mpfr_add_d(mpfr_ptr a, mpfr_srcptr b, double c, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode)
divergence_from_c: C takes (mpfr_ptr a, mpfr_srcptr b, double c, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode) -- `a` is the output slot, mutated in place, and `a`'s precision drives the rounding. TS takes (b, c, prec, rnd) and returns Result {value, ternary}. The double `c` is passed as a bare JS `number` on the TS side (per the get_d/set_d convention); the wire codec emits +0, -0, +Inf, -Inf, NaN as quoted sentinel strings to satisfy JSON's no-NaN constraint. NaN/Inf doubles propagate through mpfr_set_d to mpfr_add, which handles the standard MPFR specials cascade (NaN +/- anything = NaN; +/-Inf +/- +/-Inf with opposite signs = NaN; etc.). The MPFR_SAVE_EXPO / MPFR_CLEAR_FLAGS / mpfr_check_range pattern in the C reference manages global emax/emin state -- the TS port has no such global state (validators run per-call), so we drop the wrapper but preserve the inner ordering.
refs: mpfr/src/add_d.c L25-L50 -- the C reference body.
src/ops/add.ts -- load-bearing delegate after double->MPFR conversion.
src/ops/set_d.ts -- load-bearing for the double->MPFR step.
src/core.ts L19-L63 -- value model; double-as-string wire codec.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: NaN != NaN' -- NaN double input propagates through mpfr_add as NaN MPFR output.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: Signed zero is real' -- +0 vs -0 doubles produce distinct MPFR zeros, which then participate in mpfr_add's standard signed-zero arithmetic.
eval/golden_master/common.h jl_kv_double -- quoted-string wire form for IEEE 754 specials.
